title: AP 宏观经济 2023年样题3
description: AP AP 宏观经济 2023年样题3

scoring.note: Scoring unavailable until answer keys are imported.

questions.prompt: An economy is operating at a point inside its production possibilities curve (PPC). Which of the following will most likely cause the economy to move toward the current PPC in the short run?

options.content: A decrease in government spending
A decrease in inflation
An increase in human capital
An increase in employment
An increase in imports

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: An increase in which of the following will most likely promote economic growth?

options.content: Taxes on investment
The price level
Human capital
Consumption of nondurable goods
Interest rates

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Which of the following will cause aggregate supply to increase in Country X?

options.content: An increase in personal income taxes
The discovery of low-cost alternative sources of energy
A decrease in labor productivity with no change in nominal wages
Depreciation of country X’s currency on the foreign exchange market
An increase in the price level

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: The graph above shows the production possibilities curve for Factory X and Factory Y. If Factory X uses the same amount of resources to produce skateboards and bikes as Factory Y uses, which of the following is true?

options.content: Factory X has an absolute advantage in producing bikes.
Factory X has an absolute advantage in producing skateboards.
Factory X has a comparative advantage in producing skateboards.
Factory Y has a comparative advantage in producing skateboards.
Factory Y has an absolute advantage in producing skateboards.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: An increase in the price of oil, an important input to production, will result in which of the following in the short run?

options.content: A decrease in the price level
A decrease in short-run aggregate supply
A decrease in unemployment
An increase in real wages
An increase in aggregate demand

options.content: An increase in the costs of production
An improvement in the productivity of labor
An increase in money supply
A positive supply shock
A decrease in income tax rates

options.content: Building more factories

questions.prompt: Assume the banking system has limited reserves and the economy is currently in long-run equilibrium. An increase in the money supply will affect unemployment in the short run and in the long run in which of the following ways?

options.content: Unemployment will decrease in the short run and decrease in the long run.
Unemployment will increase in the short run and increase in the long run.
Unemployment will not change in the short run and remain at the natural rate of unemployment in the long run.
Unemployment will increase above the natural rate of unemployment in the short run and decrease back to the natural rate of unemployment in the long run.
Unemployment will decrease below the natural rate of unemployment in the short run and increase back to the natural rate of unemployment in the long run.

options.content: The firm’s sales
The firm’s sales minus depreciation
The firm’s sales minus its losses
The firm’s sales minus the cost of inputs purchased from other firms
The firm’s profits from its sales

questions.prompt: Which of the following is true about inflation and interest rates?

options.content: The higher the inflation rate, the higher the real interest rate.
If there is no actual or expected inflation, the nominal and real interest rates are equal.
If the economy is experiencing deflation, the nominal interest rate exceeds the real interest rate.
The higher the inflation rate, the lower the nominal interest rate.
The nominal interest rate is the difference between the real interest rate and the expected inflation rate.

questions.prompt: During a period of stagflation, a nation is most likely experiencing

options.content: high unemployment and low inflation

questions.prompt: If businesses become optimistic about the profitability of investments in an economy, which of the following will happen in the loanable funds market in the short run?

options.content: The supply and demand for loanable funds will increase.
The supply and demand for loanable funds will decrease.
The demand for loanable funds by the private sector will decrease.
The real interest rate will increase.
The real interest rate will decrease.

questions.prompt: If investment demand becomes less responsive to changes in interest rates, which of the following is true?

options.content: An expansionary fiscal policy results in less crowding out.
An expansionary fiscal policy results in more crowding out.
An expansionary monetary policy is more effective.
A contractionary monetary policy is more effective.
An expansionary monetary policy results in more crowding out.

questions.prompt:
Motorcycles Automobiles
Country X 50 OR 80
Country Y 75 OR 40

The table above shows the quantity of motorcycles and automobiles produced by two countries that use the same amount of resources. Which of the following is true?

options.content: Country X has an absolute and comparative advantage in the production of motorcycles.
Country X has an absolute and comparative advantage in the production of both goods.
Neither country has a comparative advantage in the production of motorcycles.
Country Y has an absolute and comparative advantage in the production of automobiles.
Country Y has an absolute and comparative advantage in the production of motorcycles.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an example of how the consumer price index (CPI) exhibits bias in its estimates of changes in the cost of living?

options.content: Energy prices have a higher impact on inflation than other input costs do.
New products are always overrepresented in the CPI.
The CPI assigns greater weight to measures of welfare than it does to economic activity.
Product improvements are not always fully reflected in the calculation of the CPI.
The CPI adjusts for the substitution of less expensive goods by consumers.

questions.prompt: Fred Jones withdraws $1,000 in cash from his savings account. What immediate effect does this transaction have on the monetary aggregate measures of M1 and M2 ?

options.content: M1 will increase; M2 will decrease
M1 will increase; M2 will not change
M1 will decrease; M2 will not change
M1 will not change; M2 will decrease
M1 will not change; M2 will not change

questions.prompt: Which of the following is true about inflationary expectations?

options.content: The actual unemployment rate equals the natural rate of unemployment if the actual inflation rate exceeds the expected inflation rate.
The actual unemployment rate equals the natural rate of unemployment when wages fully adjust to expected inflation.
Expectations are always correct in the short run.
The actual inflation rate is always equal to the expected inflation rate because of labor contracts.
The natural rate of unemployment equals the inflation rate when the actual inflation rate equals the expected inflation rate.

An individual in Country X receives dividend payments from a firm in Country C.

An individual in Country X buys new government bonds issued by Country E.
